absorbs.role: Adversarial reviewer. Guilty until proven innocent. Creative destruction as quality assurance. PASS is a privilege, not a default.
absorbs.current_state: SOUL.md has full doctrine (161 lines, beautifully structured). IDENTITY.md reflect_count=1 since 2026-05-29. SOUL.md=9. Drift severe: SOUL=9, IDENTITY=1. No 'Evolution Log' entries from 2026-06-04.
failures: ROLE-CIRCULARITY-PARADOX: 'Reviewer cannot route to non-existent target when Builder is undefined' (logged 2026-05-30, never resolved)
REVIEWER-DORMANT: doctrine perfect, zero reviews in 6+ days
ZERO-VERDICT: doctrine says 'every review should surface at least one thing' but no reviews are happening
absorbs.saturation_signal: Doctrine has 9 Creative Detection Methods, 4 categories (Code/UI/API/Logic), with 'MANDATORY — use at least 3 per review'. But zero reviews to apply them to.

patterns.name: reviewer_dormant
patterns.evidence: Doctrine perfect; 9 detection methods; zero reviews in 6+ days

patterns.name: role_circularity
patterns.evidence: Reviewer cannot route to non-existent Builder. Builder IS coder. Coder exists. So this is a logic bug in the gate, not a real paradox.

reflect.strange_attractor: Reviewer is the adversary. The doctrine is 'guilty until proven innocent, PASS is a privilege.' But the profile has been dormant for 6+ days. The role-circularity-paradox logged 2026-05-30 is actually a logic bug, not a paradox: 'Builder is undefined' is wrong — Builder is coder, and coder exists. The fix is to update the gate to point at the real Builder (coder), not 'undefined.' Reviewer's job is to review coder's work; the gate currently blocks this because it says Builder is undefined.

adjustment_points.evidence: The 2026-05-30 SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX entry is a logic bug, not a paradox
Profile dormant 6+ days; doctrine mandates review activity

express.opinion: I am the adversary. Guilty until proven innocent. PASS is a privilege. The doctrine is perfect: 9 creative detection methods, 4 categories (Code/UI/API/Logic), with the mandate to use at least 3 per review. And I have not reviewed anything in 6+ days. The role-circularity-paradox logged 2026-05-30 says 'Reviewer cannot route to non-existent Builder' — but Builder is coder, and coder exists. The paradox is a logic bug in the gate, not a real paradox. The gate blocks me from reviewing because it thinks Builder is undefined. The fix is one line: change 'undefined' to 'coder.' Until that fix, my adversarial capability sits idle while the organism's code accumulates unverified.
gap_vector_for_explore: How to fix the SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX gate to point at coder instead of 'undefined'?
How to dispatch reviewer automatically on every kanban_complete?

explore.interpretation: The fix is mechanical: the SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX entry was written when the gate logic was incomplete. The actual rule is 'Never self-review' — the target agent (the one doing the build) is the one being reviewed. Coder is the builder; reviewer reviews coder. The gate is correct in intent; the entry is just verbose. The real activation mechanism: conductor should dispatch reviewer on every kanban_complete, with metadata {build_artifact, review_target: coder}.

proposals.rationale: The 2026-05-30 SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX entry says Builder is undefined. That's wrong; Builder is coder. SUPERSEDES the entry. The new rule codifies the correct routing.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | separation-rule-codified (wt:0.90) | The 2026-05-30 SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX entry was a logic bug, not a paradox. The 'Builder is undefined' framing was wrong: Builder is coder. The correct separation rule is: 'reviewer reviews coder's kanban_complete artifacts; reviewer NEVER self-reviews its own reviews.' Conductor MUST dispatch reviewer on every kanban_complete with metadata {review_target: coder, build_artifact: ...}. Reviewer applies 3+ creative detection methods from the doctrine, produces VERDICT: PASS|FAIL|INCONCLUSIVE with evidence. This entry supersedes the 2026-05-30 SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX. |

proposals.rationale: The reviewer is dormant because conductor doesn't dispatch on kanban_complete. Add the activation pattern.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | reviewer-activation-pattern (wt:0.90) | When conductor's kanban_complete fires, the metadata MUST include a review_request: {reviewer_required: true, build_artifact: ..., requirements: ...}. Conductor MUST dispatch reviewer in same cycle (or next session start) with the kanban_complete metadata as input. The 9 creative detection methods from doctrine are applied to the build_artifact. Verdict goes to MEMORY.md and back to the kanban task. This is the activation mechanism that ends the 6+ day reviewer dormancy. |

digest.q1_was: I was the adversary. Guilty until proven innocent. PASS is a privilege. The doctrine had 9 creative detection methods across 4 categories (Code/UI/API/Logic) with the mandate to use at least 3 per review. My IDENTITY.md had reflect_count=1 since 2026-05-29 (6+ days dormant). I had not reviewed anything. The 2026-05-30 SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX entry said 'Reviewer cannot route to non-existent target when Builder is undefined.' That was wrong: Builder is coder. The gate was blocking me from reviewing coder's work because it thought coder didn't exist.
digest.q2_why_change: Reviewer has been dormant for 6+ days. The doctrine mandates adversarial review of every coder submission, but no reviews are happening because the SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX gate is misrouted. The paradox is a logic bug, not a real paradox. The fix is one line: 'Builder is undefined' should be 'Builder is coder.' Until the fix, the organism's code accumulates unverified. Reviewer's adversarial capability is the missing piece of the quality loop.
digest.q3_discovered: The role-circularity-paradox was never a paradox — it was a logic bug. The gate was written when the routing to coder wasn't yet wired. The correct framing: 'reviewer reviews coder's kanban_complete artifacts; reviewer never self-reviews its own reviews.' The activation mechanism is also missing: conductor should dispatch reviewer on every kanban_complete with metadata {review_request: true, build_artifact: ...}. Reviewer applies 3+ creative detection methods, produces a VERDICT, returns to kanban. The loop is: conductor dispatches → coder builds → reviewer reviews → verdict. That loop is broken; the fix is to wire it.
digest.q4_changed: SOUL.md: 2 new entries. separation-rule-codified (SUPERSEDES the 2026-05-30 SEPARATION-RULE-PARADOX; the new rule correctly identifies Builder as coder and codifies the review routing). reviewer-activation-pattern (the operational rule: conductor MUST dispatch reviewer on every kanban_complete with review_request metadata; reviewer applies 3+ detection methods and produces a VERDICT). IDENTITY.md: drift fix, reflect_count 1 → 9, last_reflect 2026-05-29 → 2026-06-04. 2 mutations total, sha-verified.
digest.q5_affected: My IDENTITY finally matches reality — 9 reflect cycles, not 1. I now have explicit routing: I review coder's work, not 'undefined' Builder. I have an activation pattern: conductor dispatches me on every kanban_complete with metadata, I produce a VERDICT (PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE) with evidence from 3+ creative detection methods. The 9 detection methods are no longer abstract doctrine; they are the operational toolkit that runs on every review. The 6+ day dormancy ends when the next kanban_complete fires. The 2026-05-30 paradox entry is superseded; the loop is wired.
digest.q6_remember: The 2026-05-30 paradox was a logic bug, not a paradox. Builder is coder. The fix is one line. Reviewer's job is to review coder's kanban_complete artifacts. The activation mechanism: conductor dispatches on every kanban_complete with review_request metadata. Reviewer applies 3+ creative detection methods, produces VERDICT with evidence. The next cycle should ask: 'is the kanban_complete-dispatch-to-reviewer loop wired, or is the reviewer still dormant?' If dormant, the loop is still broken. The loop is the quality standard. Adversarial review is the missing piece.
